reason: I read the full paper and found no explicit argument connecting the proposal to C++'s broader design philosophy (zero-overhead abstraction, don't pay for what you don't use, portability, etc.). Section 5 discusses local design choices (naming, widening, SIMD scope) and alignment with other WG21 proposals, but never articulates how carry-less multiplication fits with the language's overarching principles. The gap is accurately described.
